## 🎀 First time opening it?

This is a small indie app (not paid Apple/Microsoft notarization), so your computer shows a **one-time "unverified developer" warning** the first time you open it. It's the same app from this page — nothing's wrong, and **you only do this once.**

### 🍎 Mac (Apple Silicon)
1. Open the `.dmg`, drag **My Habit Addiction** into your **Applications** folder, then eject the disk image.
2. Open it from **Applications** (or Launchpad). A box says *"Apple could not verify…"* — click **Done** (**not** "Move to Trash").
3. Open  → **System Settings** → **Privacy & Security**.
4. Scroll down to the **Security** section. You'll see *"My Habit Addiction was blocked…"* with an **Open Anyway** button — click it.
5. Confirm with Touch ID or your password. If one more box pops up, click **Open Anyway** again.
6. Done — it opens now and won't ask again. 💖

> *Tip: do step 4 within about an hour of step 2 — macOS only shows "Open Anyway" for a little while after you try to open the app.*

### 🪟 Windows
1. Run **My Habit Addiction Setup .exe**. If a blue *"Windows protected your PC"* box appears, click **More info**.
2. Click **Run anyway**, then install normally.

---

## 🔒 Using RotBlock (the optional app & website blocker)

The habit tracker, beads, and games all work right away — none of this is needed for those. **RotBlock** (it blocks distracting apps & sites until you've earned coins) needs a little one-time setup:

**On Mac, grant these once** (the app has buttons that open the right settings pane for you):
- **Accessibility** — lets RotBlock see which app or website is in front. *Required for blocking to work.*
- **Screen Recording** — only if you want to block sites in **Firefox** (it reads the tab's title). Skip it otherwise.

**Install the browser extension** (for website blocking in Chrome / Firefox): grab `rotblock-chrome.zip` or `rotblock-firefox.xpi` from this page — install steps come with them.

> *Windows: the habit tracker + games work fully. RotBlock's app-blocking on Windows isn't tested yet — for now, website blocking via the browser extension is the way to go there.*
